Ready to bring people, agreements, and information together in Workspaces?
text:
[Contact Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales "Contact Sales")

![A man in a mustard-colored shirt sits indoors, engaged in a discussion about Docusign. Green plants are visible in the background.](https://images.ctfassets.net/0jnmtsdzg6p5/1yPtCtKb9PSZ82BekW4XzV/faba2ae818ce7c1cb521a18b989b78e2/male-in-orange-smiling-US-CA-AU-FR.png?fm=avif&q=90&w=500)

title: Filter and Sort Workspaces
text:
Learn how to filter and sort Workspaces to locate those that you
want to interact with.

Filter and sort Workspaces to locate the ones you want to work with. You
can filter by owner, creation time frame, and status. You can sort the results
alphabetically and by oldest and newest dates.

1. Select
   Workspaces
    from the
   Agreements page.

   A list of Workspaces displays.
2. Select all or some of the following options to narrow your search:

   | Option | Description |
   | --- | --- |
   | Search Workspaces | Start entering text and see matching results as you type. You can search by owner name and part of the workspace name. |
   | Created | View Workspaces that were created in the past 30, 60, or 90 days. |
   | Owned by | Select a user and select Apply to see Workspaces owned by specific people. |
   | All Workspaces | Select Active or Closed and select Apply to narrow the results to a subset of all Workspaces. |

Select Clear to reset the search
criteria.

3. (Optional) Sort the results by name or created date by selecting the column
   headings NAME and CREATED.

title: Workspace Templates
text:
Read about how Workspace templates help you standardize repeated processes by pre-defining envelopes, upload requests, and documents.

Workspace templates contain pre-configured envelope templates, upload requests, and documents for repeated use.

Using a template ensures consistency and reduces the setup time required for new Workspaces.

Administrators and accounts with the right permissions can use and create Workspace templates.

title: Branding in Workspaces
text:
As an administrator, you can customize the sending and signing experience to promote your brand. You can apply your brand logo and colors to email notifications and the participant portal.

When branding is applied to Signing, participants will see a customized email template that reflects your unique colors and company logo.

The Participant portal can reflect your logo, custom header background, text color, button background & text color.

External participant portal has three new notification templates (Activation, Additional Task, and Reminders ). Each one of these templates must adhere to the email branding that is specified by the Admin of the sending account.

title: Limitations on Docusign Workspaces Usage
text:
See information about limits associated with Workspaces
usage.

The following table lists the limitations that exist for Workspaces
usage. If you exceed these limits, you might encounter performance issues.

Note: eSignature envelopes are subject to the standard Docusign envelope limitations listed on the following web page:
[Limitations When Creating or Signing an Envelope
in Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Limitations-When-Creating-or-Signing-an-Envelope-in-DocuSign).

| Type of usage | Maximum |
| --- | --- |
| Maximum storage size per workspace | 1 GB |
| Maximum number of workspaces per user (per month)\* | 100 |
| Number of envelopes or files per workspace (per month)\* | 1,000 |
| File size | 50 MB |
| Number of Workspaces API calls (per month)\* | 1,000,000 |
| Number of Workspaces API calls (per hour)\* | 1000 (configurable to go higher) |
| \* Limit per customer account | |

title: Change the Status of Workspaces
text:
Learn how to change the status of a workspace from
Active to Closed when the activities
associated with it are complete.

A workspace can have a status of either Active
or Closed. When your agreement process is complete, you can
change the status to Closed to remove the workspace from the list of active Workspaces.

Note: If necessary, you can change the status back to
Active by using the same procedure.

1. Open the workspace that you want to close.
2. Select the overflow menu (the three dots next to the Get
   signatures button).
3. Select Change status > Closed > Save.

The status of the workspace is
Closed.

title: Document Upload Requests
text:
Read about how workspace preparers and
managers can request documents from collaborators in a workspace.

As a preparer or manager of a workspace, you can
request documents from collaborators through an Upload Request. Both internal and
external collaborators can receive a request, which consists of a title, optional
description, and a due date.

When you finish completing the request, you can either send it immediately or save it
to the workspace to send later with other tasks. See the topic
[Batch-Send for Envelopes and Upload Requests](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bmb1723842049882&topicId=pyq1743623833366.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how workspace preparers and managers send multiple tasks, such as envelopes and upload requests, at one time by using batch send.") to learn more.

The recipient of the Upload Request receives an email notification with a link
to view the request and other tasks. The recipient can view the due date on the request.
If the due date has passed, the request reflects a status of past due or expired.

You can assign an Upload Request to only one user at a time. To request the
same documents from different users, create separate requests.

title: Identity Verification
text:
Read about how Workspaces provide identity verification so that you can ensure individuals are authorized to access documents and agreements within a workspace.

Workspaces provides you with the ability to verify the identity of your clients per regulatory mandates, such as Know Your Customer (KYC) requirements. Many financial services companies must verify a participant’s identity before the participant accesses a workspace to protect documents and agreements within a transaction.

Table 1. ID Verification methods

| Verification Method | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Verification of government-issued IDs for simple and advanced signatures﻿. | Captures recipients' government-issued ID and verifies their identity. | - Employee onboarding - Verification of customer's identity - Bank account opening - Loan Agreements - Patient consent forms |
| Liveness detection for ID verification of government-issued IDs for simple and advanced signatures﻿. | Adds liveness detection to the standard verification of government-issued IDs. Recipients must scan their ID. Next, they must record a selfie video and conduct liveness challenges to verify their identity. | - Account opening under AML (Anti-Money Laundering) and KYC (Know   Your Customer) requirements - Access to personal medical records |
| Verification of government-issued IDs for European qualified signatures. | Adds liveness detection and manual transaction review to the standard government-issued ID verification method. This method targets customers who want to generate European qualified electronic signatures (QES). | - Signature of notarized legal documents - Digital access to government services - Temporary worker contracts - Commercial lease contracts |
| Verification Using CLEAR﻿. | Verifies identity of U.S recipients using their CLEAR account information. | - Loan applications - Patient onboarding - Customer onboarding |
| Knowledge-based authentication (KBA)﻿ | Checks recipients' identity with questions based on their public records, such as their home address. | - Medical insurance claim submission - Digital consent signature - Loan agreements - Retirement investment agreements - Bank account changes |
| Phone Authentication | Authenticates recipients with a one-time passcode (OTP) allowing them to continue the process. | - Board meeting minutes - Employment agreements - Non-Disclosure Agreements |

A workspace owner can configure the workspace to require ID verification for a participant to access the workspace. The owner can also choose a specific verification method.

The certificate of completion is updated when an envelope within the workspace includes identity verification that uses the same configuration used by the IDV required in the workspace.

For more information about using Identity Verification, see the following resources:

- [User Guide](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&rsc_301&bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=zfu1578456528197.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Sending Envelopes with ID Verification](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&rsc_301&bundleId=ced1643229641057&topicId=lyp1578456530647.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Docusign University course: Configure eSignature Identity Verification Preferences](https://dsuemployees.docusign.com/configure-esignature-identity-verification-preferences/748979/scorm/dgiqd2j51uvl)
- [Docusign University course: Support Compliance with Digital Signatures](https://dsuemployees.docusign.com/support-compliance-with-digital-signatures)
- [Docusign University video: Docusign eSignature: How to Sign with an Advanced or Qualified eSignature in the European Union](https://bcove.video/2Uij0QF)

title: Add Envelopes to Workspaces
text:
Learn how to add envelopes to a workspace. You can either create a new envelope or create an envelope from a template.

To add envelopes to a workspace, either create one or use a template.

1. Open a workspace that you want to add envelopes to.
2. Select Add > Envelope.
3. Select one of these options.

   - Create an envelope

     Create a new envelope with documents from your computer or cloud storage provider. You can add documents from Box, Dropbox, and Google Drive.
   - Use a template

     Select a template that you created or that is shared with you.
4. Enter the information for the recipient or recipients of your envelope.
5. Select Save to workspace to save the envelope as a draft and return to the workspace, or select Get signatures to add recipients and send the documents for signature immediately.

title: Messaging in Workspaces
text:
Read about how you can collaborate with members of a workspace through messaging.

Messaging is a key collaboration tool offered by Workspaces.

Workspace preparers can exchange messages with other members of the workspace, including their external clients. External clients can access messaging through the participant portal link that is emailed to them and do not require a Docusign account.

Workspace members who receive an envelope or upload request can directly communicate with workspace preparers by sending and receiving messages through the participant portal.

Note:

Administrators can disable messaging for an account. For more information, see [Disable Messaging](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bmb1723842049882&topicId=tsk9d79ef25-a585-4793-a236-c39c76046824.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how administrators disable messaging for all users and workspaces in an account.").

title: Add Documents to Workspaces
text:
Learn how to add documents to a workspace. You can add
documents by uploading them from a cloud storage provider, such as Box, Dropbox, and Google
Drive. You can also add them through templates.

You can add documents by uploading them from local or cloud storage, such as Box, Dropbox, and
Google Drive, or by uploading templates.

1. Open a workspace that you want to add documents
   to.
2. Select Add > Document.

   The Add documents page opens.
3. Add documents from one of the following sources.
   - Use template Add templates that you have created or templates
     that are shared with you.
   - Upload Add one or more documents from your computer or a cloud
     storage provider, such as Box, Dropbox, or Google Drive.

The document you selected displays in the Add documents
page.

4. Continue repeating step 3 to add the documents you want.
5. Select Save and close to return to the workspace, or select Get signatures
   to add recipients and send the documents for signature.

   When you select Get signatures for a document, the
   document becomes part of the envelope and is no longer displayed separately in
   the workspace.

title: Add Users to Workspaces
text:
Learn how to add users to Workspaces. You can add users both within
and outside of your organization. However, the level of permissions available differs
depending on whether the user is on the same account as the Workspaces
creator.

You can add users to Workspaces so they can upload documents and complete
agreement steps. You can assign a user one of two permission levels: Manage and
Participate.

Note: Only users within the organization can have Manage level permission
assigned to them. To have Manage level permission, the user you add must be on the
same account as the person who created the workspace. Users
external to your organization are restricted to Participate permission only.

1. Open a workspace to which you want to add a user.
2. Select Share.
3. Type the first name, family name, and email address for the user you want to
   add.
4. (Optional) Add users by selecting Add New Person.
5. Select Done.

The user or users you added are listed as participants in the
workspace. Internal users receive an email invitation.
External users receive an email notification when they are assigned tasks.

title: Add Users to Workspaces
text: You can assign actions and share documents with the user you added.

title: Download Message History
text:
Learn how to download a CSV file of message history.

You can download the message history for a given topic in a CSV format. This file contains
information, such as transaction ID, participant ID, export time stamp, and message content and time stamps.

1. Select the Messages icon in the upper right.
2. Select an existing topic from the left panel.
3. Select the download icon in the upper right.

A CSV file is downloaded to your computer.

title: View the History of Workspaces
text:
Learn how to view information about the history of a workspace.
You can view its creation date, the name of the user who created it, and details about
changes made to the envelopes that it contains.

View information about the life of a workspace, such as its creation
date and actions taken by users on it, through the View
history option.

1. Open a workspace by selecting
   Workspaces
    from the
   Agreements page.
2. Select View history from the overflow menu (the three
   stacked dots next to Get Signatures at the top of the
   page).

   Information about the life of the workspace displays in
   columns by date and time, user, action, and activity.
3. Select the X to close the history window and return to
   the workspace.

title: Share a Document in Workspaces
text:
Learn how owners and managers of Workspaces share documents with
other users both within and outside of the organization. Users can be added as participants
to all documents in the workspace or be given
access to specific agreement items.

You can share a document in a workspace with
collaborators that have been invited or with users that are not a part of the
workspace.

1. Open a workspace from the
   Workspaces
    page.
2. Locate the document that you want to share and select
   Share from the overflow menu (the three stacked dots
   next to View).

   The Share document with others dialog box
   opens.
3. Enter the email address for the user that you want to add and select
   Share.

   The participant you added is now visible beneath People with access to
   this document in the Share document with
   others dialog box.
4. Close the dialog box and return to the workspace.

The user you added receives a notification with a link to
the document.

title: Share a Document in Workspaces
text:
After sharing a document, you can assign actions to the
participant.

title: Actions Permitted By Permission Level and Role
heading_path: Actions Permitted By Permission Level and Role
text:
Read about the actions that users who are invited to Workspaces or
have documents shared with them can take, depending on their role.

title: Actions Permitted By Permission Level and Role
heading_path: Actions Permitted By Permission Level and Role
text:
The role that individuals take when interacting with Workspaces
depends on their association with the Docusign account that contains the workspace.

Workspaces owner
:   The creator of the workspace.

Internal invitee
:   Individual invited to the workspace who is a member of the same
    Docusign account as the Workspaces owner.

External invitee
:   Individual invited to the workspace who is not a member of the
    same Docusign account as the Workspaces owner.

Internal or external non-invitee
:   Individual who is not invited to a workspace but is associated
    with the tasks or documents within the workspace. For example, an individual
    that is assigned an upload request.

title: Actions Permitted By Permission Level and Role
heading_path: Actions Permitted By Permission Level and Role
text:
Individuals who are invited to Workspaces can
be assigned either **Manage** or **Participate** permissions.

Manage
:   The Manage permission level allows adding and removing participants from a Workspace.
    Only invitees that are members of the same Docusign account as the
    Workspaces creator
    can have this permission.

    Note: Managing tasks or envelopes within a workspace is restricted to the owner of those artifacts.

Participate
:   The Participate permission can be assigned to invitees
    that are either members or non-members of the Docusign account. This
    permission limits the actions that assignees can perform.

title: Actions Permitted By Permission Level and Role
heading_path: Actions Permitted By Permission Level and Role
Actions allowed by permission level and role
text:
The following table lists the actions allowed for each permission level by role.

| Action | Workspaces owner | Internal invitee (Manage access) | Internal invitee (Participate access) | External invitee (Participate access) | Internal and External non-invitee |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Add users to the workspace | Y (Only those created by the owner) | Y | N | N | N |
| View envelopes | Y | Y (only envelopes received) | Y (Only envelopes received) | Y (Only envelopes received) | Y (Only envelopes received) |
| Sign envelopes | Y (Only envelopes received) | Y (only envelopes received) | Y (Only envelopes received) | Y (Only envelopes received) | Y (Only envelopes received) |
| Create envelopes | Y | Y | N | N | N |
| Send envelopes | Y | Y | N | N | N |
| Revise and void envelopes | Y (Only envelopes created by the owner) | Y (Only envelopes created by the invitee) | N | N | N |
| Upload documents | Y | Y | Y | Y | N |
| Send workspace documents in envelope | Y (Only documents created by the owner) | Y (Only documents created by the invitee) | N | N | N |
| Share documents | Y | Y | Y | Y (Only documents uploaded by the invitee) | N |
| View documents | Y | Y | Y (Only documents uploaded by or shared with the invitee) | Y (Only documents uploaded by or shared with the invitee) | Y (Only documents uploaded by or shared with the non-invitee) |
| Rename documents | Y | Y | Y (Only documents uploaded by the invitee) | Y (Only documents uploaded by the invitee) | N |
| Delete documents | Y (Only documents created by the owner) | Y (Only documents uploaded by the invitee) | Y (Only documents uploaded by the invitee) | Y (Only documents uploaded by the invitee) | N |
| View users | Y | Y | N | N | N |
| Add upload request | Y | Y | N | N | N |
| Batch-send tasks (envelopes and upload requests) | Y | Y | N | N | N |
| Send a message | Y | Y | Y | N | N |
| View workspace history | Y | Y | N | N | N |

title: Docusign Workspaces
text:
Read about how you can use Docusign
Workspaces to manage multiple agreements and their data throughout the
life of a transaction.

Workspaces can help you manage multiple agreements and their data
throughout the life of an agreement more easily.

Workspaces improves collaboration with others both within and outside of
your organization. Invite users to a workspace so they can add
and review documents, sign envelopes, and monitor overall progress. Depending on how
much interaction you want them to have, you can assign different level permissions.

With Workspaces, it is easy to view and complete envelopes and keep track
of notifications. When you send envelopes from Workspaces, signers
receive a single email that directs them to a centralized signing experience that lists
all notifications.

title: Participant Portal
text:
Read about how the participant portal enables workspace collaborators to access and
complete requests.

Tasks that you send to workspace collaborators, such as requests for documents or
signature, are viewed and acted on in the participant portal.

When you send tasks to a participant, the participant receives an email notification that
links directly to the participant portal. The portal displays all assigned tasks, with
due dates, if available, and includes links to take action.

The participant does not need a Docusign account to access the
participant portal to view or complete the assigned tasks. As tasks are completed, the
status of each is updated in the workspace for easy tracking.

As the preparer of a workspace, you can also add a personalized message to the participant portal, which appears at the top of the portal. The message appears at the workspace level and is visible to all recipients that use the portal.

title: Batch-Send for Envelopes and Upload Requests
heading_path: Batch-Send for Envelopes and Upload Requests
text:
Read about how workspace preparers and managers send multiple
tasks, such as envelopes and upload requests, at one time by using batch send.

As a workspace preparer or manager, you can consolidate
multiple tasks, such as envelopes and upload requests, and send them as a batch. Batch
sending is efficient not only for you as a preparer or manager, but also for recipients.
When you send tasks in a batch, recipients receive a single notification that
consolidates the multiple tasks.

To batch-send envelopes and upload requests, you save them to the workspace instead of sending them immediately after creation. They
remain in draft status until you select them from the Workspaces
detail view and send them together.

For more information, see the topic [Send Multiple Envelopes and Upload Requests in a Batch](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bmb1723842049882&topicId=xix1741909658346.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to send multiple envelopes and upload requests together in a batch.").

title: Sharing Workspaces with Other Users
text:
Read about how you can share a workspace and documents within a
workspace with other users by assigning roles.

You can invite users to collaborate in Workspaces to add
documents and complete agreement steps.

The role you assign to users determines the level they can interact with the workspace. You can assign one of two roles: Manage and Participate.

- Manage–Users can view all documents in the workspace, view
  completed envelopes, add and share documents, and invite other collaborators.

  Note: The Manage role can be assigned to internal users only. To
  have this role, users must be on the same account as the person who created the
  workspace.
- Participate–Users can view and complete agreement steps assigned to them. You can assign this
  role to both internal and external users.

  Note: External users
  are those who are not on the same account as the workspace creator. These users can have the Participate role
  only. When you invite external users to a workspace,
  the Participate role is presented as the only option.

title: Send Messages to Workspaces Users
text: Learn how to send messages to users of a workspace.

title: Send Messages to Workspaces Users
text:
Ensure that the users you want to message have been added to the workspace. You can add users in the
following ways:

- Share the workspace with a user
- Assign a task to a user

Workspaces offers a messaging feature that enhances collaboration between
workspace users. The messages you send are
consolidated within the workspace for easy reference.

1. Open the workspace.
2. Select the View messages icon in the upper right.
3. Select New Message.

   The Create New Message dialog box displays.
4. (Optional) Enter a topic name of 100 characters or less.
5. Select a user from the Workspace participants menu.
6. Select Create.
7. Enter a message in the message field and select the arrow icon to send.

The message you sent displays in the workspace messages view.

title: Create Workspaces
text:
Learn how to create a workspace, to manage documents and
collaborate with other users.

Create a workspace to manage
documents and collaborate with other users.

1. Select the Agreements tab.
2. Select
   Workspaces
    from the left
   navigation area.
3. Enter a name for the workspace and select
   Save.

   A window with your new workspace opens and
   presents you with options to get signatures, invite collaborators, or add
   documents.

title: Create Workspaces
text:
Send your document for signature or request documents from
another user.

title: Download Documents from Workspaces
text:
Learn how to save copies of documents from your workspace to
your local environment for storage.

When you want to retain a copy of documents in a workspace, you can
download them to your computer.

1. Open a workspace by selecting
   Workspaces
    from the
   Agreements page.
2. Select the overflow menu (the three stacked dots next to Get
   Signatures).
3. Select Download all.

   The Download dialog box opens.
4. (Optional) If you do not want to download all documents, select the option for the document
   type you want to download, and select Download.

The documents are downloaded as a ZIP file.

title: Using a Workspace Template
text: Learn how to create a new Workspace using a published template.

title: Using a Workspace Template
text:
Once a Workspace template is published, it becomes available for you and others on your account to use. Using a template automatically populates your new Workspace with the pre-defined items from the template.

1. From the Home or Agreements page, select Start > Workspaces > Use a Workspace Template.

   The Use a workspace template page opens.

   Note: You can also access Workplace templates from the Templates page by selecting Workspace Templates. Locate the template you'd like to use and select Use.
2. Locate the template you want to use and select Use.

   The Name the workspace window opens.
3. Enter a name for the new Workspace and select Save.

The Workspace is created with the structure and items defined in the template.

title: Add a Personalized Message to a Workspace
heading_path: Add a Personalized Message to a Workspace
text: Learn how to add a custom message visible to all envelope recipients within a Workspace.

title: Add a Personalized Message to a Workspace
heading_path: Add a Personalized Message to a Workspace
text:
You can add a personalized message to your Workspace to provide instructions, greetings, or context. This ensures that every recipient who accesses a task within that Workspace views the same message.

1. From the Agreements page, select Workspaces from the left navigation.

   The Workspaces page opens.
2. Select an existing Workspace or [create a new Workspace](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bmb1723842049882&topicId=sha1723842408338.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a workspace, to manage documents and collaborate with other users.").

   The Workspace Overview page opens.
3. Select Settings from the Actions menu.

   The Workspace Settings window opens.![The screenshot shows the Actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bmb1723842049882/page/images/Per_options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE1NDUsInNoZWFmIjoiYm1iMTcyMzg0MjA0OTg4MiJ9.ztxQ--qQzxMMsufRkTWA5lFocnh10uOhNq3Yo_S98zM&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Actions menu.")
4. Enter your personalized message for this Workspace in the Message to recipients field.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bmb1723842049882/page/images/WSmessage.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE1NDUsInNoZWFmIjoiYm1iMTcyMzg0MjA0OTg4MiJ9.ztxQ--qQzxMMsufRkTWA5lFocnh10uOhNq3Yo_S98zM&_LANG=enus)
5. Select Save.

Your message is saved. It now appears to all recipients in any envelope added to the Workspace.

title: Restrict Access to a Workspace Through Identity Verification
heading_path: Restrict Access to a Workspace Through Identity Verification
text: Learn how workspace owners restrict access to workspaces by external participants through identity verification checks.

title: Restrict Access to a Workspace Through Identity Verification
heading_path: Restrict Access to a Workspace Through Identity Verification
text:
You can secure a workspace by requiring that external participants pass an identity verification check before accessing it.

1. Open the workspace that you want to secure.
2. Select Share.
3. Select Add new person.
4. Enter the name and email address of the participant.
5. Select the overflow menu (the three stacked dots), and select Add Identity Verification.
6. Select the type of identity verification you want to use.
7. Select Share.

Docusign prompts the participant to perform and pass an identity verification check before allowing access to the participant portal. After passing the check, the participant is admitted to the participant portal and can access the shared workspace.

title: Disable Messaging
text: Learn how administrators disable messaging for all users and workspaces in an account.

title: Disable Messaging
text:
You must have administrator permissions to perform this task.

Disable messaging for users and workspaces in an account by following these steps:

1. Select the Admin tab.
2. Select Workspaces Settings from the left navigation.
3. Select the toggle for Enable Messaging Feature to turn messaging off.

title: Publish a Workspace Template
text: Learn how to publish a Workspace template to make it available for use.

title: Publish a Workspace Template
text:
After you finish building your Workspace template and adding all necessary items, you can publish it. Publishing makes the template visible so that you and other users can use it.

After [Creating a Workspace Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bmb1723842049882&topicId=tskdd3dde17-2ad1-4d13-a0e2-eed0edf56201.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a Workspace template to standardize the content for your recurring projects."), select Publish.

The Workspace template is published and available to use.

Note: You can update a published template. Changes are saved automatically while editing.

title: Create a Workspace Template
text: Learn how to create a Workspace template to standardize the content for your recurring projects.

title: Create a Workspace Template
text:
You must have the permissions required to create envelope templates.

Creating a Workspace template allows you to pre-define the structure for projects that you use frequently. You can add envelope templates, upload requests, and documents to the Workspace template.

1. From the Templates page, select Workspace Templates from the left navigation.

   The Workspace Templates page opens.

   Note: Alternately, access Workspace Templates from the Home tab by selecting Start > Create Workspace Template .
2. Select Create Template.

   The Create Workspace Template window opens.
3. Enter a Workspace Template Name and an optional description.![The screenshot shows the Create Workspace Template page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bmb1723842049882/page/images/CreateWT.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE0OTMsInNoZWFmIjoiYm1iMTcyMzg0MjA0OTg4MiJ9.pYdd2WjykXxLAwf1f3uoE-0aBk5kaBeFR8AqggaWacs&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Create Workspace Template page.")
4. Select Next.

   The overview page for your Workspace template opens.![The screenshot shows the workspace template overview page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bmb1723842049882/page/images/Build.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE0OTMsInNoZWFmIjoiYm1iMTcyMzg0MjA0OTg4MiJ9.pYdd2WjykXxLAwf1f3uoE-0aBk5kaBeFR8AqggaWacs&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the workspace template overview page.")
5. Add envelope templates to the Workspace.
   1. Select Add > Envelope

      The Add Templates page opens.
   2. Locate the envelope templates you want to add and select Add.

      You have added envelope templates to your Workspace template.

      Note: Only users who have permission to use the envelope template will see it in the Workspace. Envelope template access is not automatically shared when added to a Workspace template
6. Add Upload Requests to the Workspace.
   1. Select Add > Upload Request.

      The Details page opens.
   2. Complete the details on the Details page.![The screenshot shows the Upload Request page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bmb1723842049882/page/images/Upload_Request.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE0OTMsInNoZWFmIjoiYm1iMTcyMzg0MjA0OTg4MiJ9.pYdd2WjykXxLAwf1f3uoE-0aBk5kaBeFR8AqggaWacs&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Upload Request page.")
   3. Select Save.

      You added an Upload Request to your Workspace template.
7. Add documents to the Workspace.
   1. Select Add > Document.![The screenshot shows options under the Add menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bmb1723842049882/page/images/UPloadrequestmenu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MTE0OTMsInNoZWFmIjoiYm1iMTcyMzg0MjA0OTg4MiJ9.pYdd2WjykXxLAwf1f3uoE-0aBk5kaBeFR8AqggaWacs&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows options under the Add menu.")

      The Add documents page opens.
   2. Select Upload and locate the files you would like to add.
   3. Select Add.

      You have added documents to your Workplace template.

You have created a Workspace template.

title: Create a Workspace Template
text: Publish your Workspace template to make it available for others to use. Until you publish it, the template remains in a draft state and cannot be seen or used to create a new Workspace.

title: Rename Workspaces
text:
Learn how to change the name of a workspace.

You can change the name of an existing workspace.

1. Open a workspace.
2. Select the overflow menu (the three vertical dots).
3. Select Rename.
4. Edit the name and select Save.

The name of the workspace reflects the
update that you made.

title: Request Documents from Another User
text:
Learn how to send an upload request for documents to another user. Users that receive
the request can upload the requested documents to the workspace.

Create an upload request when you want to ask
another user to upload documents to the workspace.

1. Open the workspace that you want to request documents
   for.
2. Select Add > Upload Request.

   The Details page for the Upload
   Request displays.
3. You can either send the request immediately or save it to the workspace to send it later with other tasks.

The upload request you saved or sent displays in your workspace. The saved request is labeled as
Draft and the request that you sent immediately shows
Waiting for upload.

title: Send Multiple Envelopes and Upload Requests in a Batch
heading_path: Send Multiple Envelopes and Upload Requests in a Batch
text:
Learn how to send multiple envelopes and upload requests together in a
batch.

As a workspace preparer or manager, you can send multiple
envelopes and upload requests in a batch rather than sending them immediately. The
recipient receives one notification with a link to the envelopes and requests that
need attention.

1. Prepare each upload request or envelope for signature and instead of sending
   them immediately, select Save to workspace
   .

   The request or prepared envelope is saved to your workspace with a status of
   Draft.
2. When you are ready to send the upload requests, envelopes, or a combination
   from the workspace, select the check box for each.
3. Select Send and select Send again
   in the confirmation dialog box.

   The status of the envelopes changes to Waiting for
   others and the status of the upload requests changes to
   Waiting for upload.

title: Delete Workspaces
text:
Learn how to delete a workspace when you no longer need
it.

If you no longer need a workspace, you can delete it.

1. Select
   Workspaces
    from the
   Agreements page.
2. Select the check box next to the workspace that you
   want to remove.

   To see all closed and active Workspaces, select
   All Workspaces
    from the
   Status button.
3. Select Delete.

The workspace is no longer visible in the
list.
